title: The Daisy Follows Soft The Sun,
content:
The daisy follows soft the sun,
    And when his golden walk is done,
         Sits shyly at his feet.
    He, waking, finds the flower near.
    "Wherefore, marauder, art thou here?"
         "Because, sir, love is sweet!"

    We are the flower, Thou the sun!
    Forgive us, if as days decline,
         We nearer steal to Thee, --
    Enamoured of the parting west,
    The peace, the flight, the amethyst,
         Night's possibility!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "The Daisy Follows Soft The Sun,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
